Tested through CL's Quality Certification Program prior to, or after initial posting of this Product Review.

* Product identical in formulation and manufacture to a product that has passed testing but sold under a different brand. For more information see CL's Multi-Label Testing Program.
** Caution — These compounds, although naturally occurring, may have a laxative effect and are potential carcinogens. They are expected in aloe latex and whole leaf products, but aloe gel products should contain no detectable emodin and aloins must be less than 10 ppm.
† Aloe ferox and aloe leaf products will naturally contain latex, which may have a laxative effect and may be carcinogenic according to the FDA.  

1 For 200:1 aloe gel extracts and dried aloe vera leaf juice, product must contain at least 5% D-acemannan by dry weight (IASC specification for raw material). 
2 Not tested but claimed on label.
3 Purchased with a buy one bottle get the second one 50% off. First bottle cost $13.99, and second cost $6.99.
4 Product purchased as buy 2 get 3 free for $35.98.
5 Based on 1 serving daily.
6 No acemannan detected, but results were obscured and inconclusive.
7 The lab reported no acemannan detected, so value could not be calculated.
8 The lab reported only traces of acemannan detected, so value could not be calculated.
9 The expected minimum was calculated by multiplying the claimed amount of juice (36 mL) by the density (1,019 mg/mL), then by 0.5% (the expected percent solids of single strength aloe juice) and then by 5% (the minimum expected amount of acemannan in the solids).
10 Product added on 10/13/2017.  
11Reformulated: In November 2020, a CL member notified us that the forumuation of this product has slightly changed and now lists radish root ferment filtrate instead of grapefruit extract, guar gum and tocopherols.

Unless otherwise noted, information about the products listed above is based on the samples purchased by ConsumerLab.com (CL) for this Product Review. Manufacturers may change ingredients and label information at any time, so be sure to check labels carefully when evaluating the products you use or buy. If a product's ingredients differ from what is listed above, it may not necessarily be of the same quality as what was tested.

The information contained in this report is based on the compilation and review of information from product labeling and analytic testing. CL applies what it believes to be the most appropriate testing methods and standards. The information in this report does not reflect the opinion or recommendation of CL, its officers or employees. CL cannot assure the accuracy of information.
